name: Yokohama Bay
tagline: The end of the road on the west side — wild, empty, and painted in fire every evening.
parking: Free lot at end of Farrington Hwy
hours: Open daily, 7:00 AM – 7:45 PM (gates locked at closing)
bestTime: Sunset year-round; summer for swimming (winter has dangerous surf)
talkStory: Yokohama Bay's Hawaiian name is Keawaula, meaning 'the red harbor.' It got its English name from the Japanese fishermen from Yokohama who fished these waters in the early 1900s. This is literally the end of the road on the west side — beyond here, the Ka'ena Point trail leads to Oahu's westernmost tip, a sacred site where ancient Hawaiians believed souls leapt into the afterlife. There is zero shade, no lifeguards, and winter surf is genuinely deadly. Come in summer for swimming and at sunset year-round for some of the most spectacular skies on the island.
